items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for New York's main international airport, named after the 35th U.S. president?
items.explanation:
John F. Kennedy International Airport in Queens, New York
Renamed from Idlewild Airport in 1963 after President Kennedy's assassination
One of the busiest international air gateways to the United States
Originally built in 1948 on the site of Idlewild Golf Course
The TWA Flight Center (now TWA Hotel), designed by Eero Saarinen, is a landmark of mid-century modern architecture

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the main airport serving Los Angeles, California?
items.explanation:
Los Angeles International Airport in Westchester, Los Angeles
The "X" was added because early IATA codes expanded two-letter identifiers to three; LA became LAX
The iconic Theme Building (1961), a space-age structure resembling a flying saucer, is a designated Los Angeles landmark
Busiest airport on the U.S. West Coast and a major gateway to the Pacific Rim
Served over 88 million passengers in 2023, making it the third-busiest airport in the world

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for London's largest airport, located west of the city in the borough of Hillingdon?
items.explanation:
London Heathrow Airport, the busiest airport in Europe by international passenger traffic
Code derives from the hamlet of Heath Row (two words), absorbed when the airport was built on the site of a military airfield in 1946
Has four passenger terminals; Terminal 5 is the dedicated British Airways hub
Connects to central London via the Piccadilly line and the Heathrow Express to Paddington
A proposed third runway has been debated for decades, remaining one of the most contentious infrastructure projects in British politics

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the main international airport serving Paris, named after the leader of Free France during World War II?
items.explanation:
Paris Charles de Gaulle Airport in Roissy-en-France, 23 km northeast of Paris
Named after Charles de Gaulle (1890–1970), wartime resistance leader and first president of the Fifth Republic
Opened in 1974 to relieve Orly Airport; the futuristic Terminal 1 was designed by Paul Andreu
Primary hub for Air France and the busiest airport in France
Part of Terminal 2E collapsed in 2004, just one year after opening, killing four people

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for Tokyo's primary international airport, located in Chiba Prefecture about 60 km east of central Tokyo?
items.explanation:
Narita International Airport, opened in 1978 after years of violent protests by local farmers and activists opposing land seizure
Code comes from the city of Narita, where the airport is located in Chiba Prefecture
Originally called New Tokyo International Airport; renamed Narita in 2004
Construction was delayed nearly a decade by the Sanrizuka struggle, one of the most intense anti-development movements in Japanese history
Handles most international flights for the Tokyo metropolitan area, while Haneda handles primarily domestic and some international routes

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the main airport of the Southeast Asian city-state consistently ranked among the world's best airports?
items.explanation:
Singapore Changi Airport, repeatedly voted the world's best airport by Skytrax
Code is derived from the first three letters of Singapore
The Jewel Changi complex (2019) features the world's tallest indoor waterfall, the Rain Vortex, at 40 meters
A critical hub connecting Europe, East Asia, and Oceania, handling over 65 million passengers annually
Located on reclaimed land at the eastern tip of Singapore, on the site of the former RAF Changi air base

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the main airport of the UAE city that is the busiest airport in the world by international passenger traffic?
items.explanation:
Dubai International Airport, the busiest airport in the world by international passengers since 2014
Code is an abbreviation of Dubai's transliterated Arabic name
Home base of Emirates airline, whose Terminal 3 is one of the largest airport buildings in the world by floor space
Handled over 87 million passengers in 2023
Dubai is also building Al Maktoum International (DWC), planned to eventually become the world's largest airport

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for Germany's busiest airport, a major European hub located in the Hesse region?
items.explanation:
Frankfurt Airport (Flughafen Frankfurt am Main), Germany's largest airport and a major Lufthansa hub
Code is a straightforward abbreviation of Frankfurt
The busiest airport in Germany and typically third-busiest in Europe after Heathrow and CDG
Serves as the primary gateway for transatlantic flights to continental Europe
Located adjacent to the Frankfurter Kreuz, the busiest autobahn interchange in Germany

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the main airport of Australia's largest city, located in the suburb of Mascot?
items.explanation:
Sydney Kingsford Smith Airport, named after Sir Charles Kingsford Smith, the first person to fly across the Pacific Ocean (1928)
Code is a straightforward abbreviation of Sydney
Australia's busiest airport, handling over 40 million passengers per year
Operates under a government-imposed curfew: no flights between 11 PM and 6 AM, unusual for a major international airport
Located in Mascot, only 8 km south of the Sydney CBD, making it one of the closest major airports to a city center

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for Chicago's primary airport, a major United Airlines hub on the city's northwest side?
items.explanation:
Chicago O'Hare International Airport, named after Edward "Butch" O'Hare, a U.S. Navy aviator and Medal of Honor recipient in World War II
Code comes from the airport's original name, Orchard Field (ORD), which was a Douglas aircraft factory during WWII
Was the world's busiest airport from 1963 to 1998 before being surpassed by Atlanta
A major hub for both United Airlines and American Airlines
The underground walkway between terminals B and C features a neon light installation by Michael Hayden called "Sky's the Limit"

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the airport that has been the world's busiest by total passenger traffic for most years since 1998?
items.explanation:
Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport, named after former Atlanta mayors William B. Hartsfield and Maynard Jackson
The world's busiest airport by passenger numbers for over two decades, handling over 93 million passengers in 2023
Primary hub for Delta Air Lines, the world's largest airline by revenue
The Plane Train automated people mover connects the domestic terminal to concourses and the international terminal
Originally a racetrack called the Atlanta Speedway, converted to an airfield in 1925

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for Rome's main international airport, located in the coastal town of Fiumicino?
items.explanation:
Rome Fiumicino Airport, officially Leonardo da Vinci International Airport
Code comes from Fiumicino, the municipality southwest of Rome where the airport is located
Opened in 1961 to replace Rome Ciampino as the city's primary airport
Italy's busiest airport and the main hub for ITA Airways (successor to Alitalia)
Situated near the ancient Roman port of Ostia and the archaeological site of Portus, the harbor built by Emperor Claudius

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the Tokyo airport closer to the city center, originally built in 1931 and expanded for international service?
items.explanation:
Tokyo Haneda Airport (officially Tokyo International Airport), located in Ota, Tokyo, just 14 km from Tokyo Station
Code comes from the neighborhood of Haneda where the airport was first built in 1931
Japan's busiest airport and the primary domestic hub; international flights returned in 2010 with a new terminal
Was Japan's sole international gateway until Narita opened in 1978
The expansion onto reclaimed land in Tokyo Bay added a fourth runway, dramatically increasing capacity

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the older of Beijing's two major airports, which served as the city's main hub until 2019?
items.explanation:
Beijing Capital International Airport, China's busiest airport until the opening of Beijing Daxing in 2019
Code derives from Peking, the older romanization of Beijing used when IATA codes were assigned
Terminal 3, designed by Norman Foster for the 2008 Olympics, was the world's largest airport building at the time
Primary hub for Air China
First opened in 1958, making it one of the oldest airports in China still in operation

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the main international airport serving Seoul, South Korea, built on reclaimed land between two islands?
items.explanation:
Incheon International Airport, located on Yeongjong Island, 70 km west of Seoul
Code comes from Incheon, the metropolitan city in whose jurisdiction the airport was built
Opened in 2001 to replace Gimpo Airport as Seoul's international hub
Consistently ranked among the world's best airports for service and cleanliness
Built on reclaimed tidal land between Yeongjong and Yongyu islands, connected to Seoul by expressway and the AREX rail line

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for Canada's busiest airport, located in Mississauga, Ontario, near Toronto?
items.explanation:
Toronto Pearson International Airport, named after Lester B. Pearson, Canada's 14th Prime Minister and 1957 Nobel Peace Prize laureate
Code follows the Canadian convention where most major airports start with Y; YZ was the identifier for the Malton, Ontario radio range station near the airport
Canada's largest and busiest airport, handling over 50 million passengers annually
Primary hub for Air Canada
The Rush instrumental track "YYZ" (1981) uses the airport code's Morse code rhythm (–.–– –.–– ––..) as its opening riff

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the main international airport serving São Paulo, Brazil, located in a municipality northeast of the city?
items.explanation:
São Paulo–Guarulhos International Airport, officially Aeroporto Internacional de São Paulo/Guarulhos – Governador André Franco Montoro
Code comes from Guarulhos, the municipality in Greater São Paulo where the airport is located
The busiest airport in South America and Brazil's primary international gateway
Opened in 1985 to supplement the older Congonhas Airport, which still handles domestic shuttle flights
Located 25 km northeast of São Paulo's city center in one of the most populous metropolitan areas in the Western Hemisphere

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the main Dutch airport, built on the site of a former lake bed that lies below sea level?
items.explanation:
Amsterdam Airport Schiphol, located 9 km southwest of Amsterdam in the Haarlemmermeer polder
Code is a straightforward abbreviation of Amsterdam
The name Schiphol may derive from "Scheepshol" (ship grave), referring to shipwrecks on the former Haarlemmermeer lake before it was drained in 1852
Sits approximately 3 meters below sea level, making it one of the lowest-elevation major airports in the world
A single-terminal design with a central plaza (Schiphol Plaza) connecting rail, road, and air transport, praised as one of Europe's most efficient layouts

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the main airport of Turkey's largest city, opened in 2018 as one of the world's biggest airports?
items.explanation:
Istanbul Airport, opened in October 2018 to replace Atatürk Airport as the city's main international hub
Code was transferred from the old Atatürk Airport (which used IST) when operations moved to the new facility in April 2019
Designed to eventually handle 200 million passengers annually, which would make it the highest-capacity airport ever built
Primary hub for Turkish Airlines, the carrier that flies to more countries than any other airline
Located on the European side of Istanbul, near the Black Sea coast, about 35 km northwest of the city center

items.prompt: What is the three-letter IATA code for the main airport of the southeastern Florida city that serves as the busiest U.S. gateway to Latin America and the Caribbean?
items.explanation:
Miami International Airport, the busiest U.S. airport for international freight and a top gateway for passenger travel to Latin America
Code is a straightforward abbreviation of Miami
Primary hub for American Airlines, which operates roughly 70% of all flights at the airport
Handles more passengers and cargo to and from Latin America and the Caribbean than any other U.S. airport
The airport's North Terminal (designed by Arquitectonica) features a striking curved white roof visible from the Dolphin Expressway
